Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}}Kushida 6841 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|41|31.10|N|137|2|42.22|E|display=ti}} | 祭神=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto
Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県射水市串田6841', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} [[JA:櫛田神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544725}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kushida-jinja(Imizu)_haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}Kushida 6841 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|41|31.10|N|137|2|42.22|E|display=ti}} | 祭神=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto
Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県射水市串田6841', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} [[JA:櫛田神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544725}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kushida-jinja(Imizu)_haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}Kushida 6841 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|41|31.10|N|137|2|42.22|E|display=ti}} | 祭神=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto
Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]]

(provincia)|sr|Провинција Ечу|tr|Etchū (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Еттю|vi|Tỉnh Etchū|zh_min_nan|Ettyû-kok|zh_yue|越中國|zh|越中國|WD=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}} * {{ill|Q6004195|ltq=Q6004195|WD=Q6004195|1=Imizu address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県射水市串田6841', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} [[JA:櫛田神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544725}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kushida-jinja(Imizu)_haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}Kushida 6841 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|41|31.10|N|137|2|42.22|E|display=ti}} | 祭神=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto
Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]]

A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県射水市串田6841', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} [[JA:櫛田神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544725}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kushida-jinja(Imizu)_haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}Kushida 6841 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|41|31.10|N|137|2|42.22|E|display=ti}} | 祭神=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto
Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|WD=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|WD=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|WD=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|WD=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|WD=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]]

Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|qid=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|qid=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|qid=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|qid=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|qid=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|qid=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|qid=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|qid=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|qid=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|qid=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|qid=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|qid=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|qid=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]]

street address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県射水市串田6841', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} [[JA:櫛田神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544725}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kushida-jinja(Imizu)_haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|qid=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|qid=Q851411|1=Imizu}}Kushida 6841 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|41|31.10|N|137|2|42.22|E|display=ti}} | 祭神=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto
Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=September 10|Q2853|qid=Q2853|1=September 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Shinkawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|qid=Q851411|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|qid=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. It is worshiped as a deity of marital harmony, family harmony, and matchmaking. === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Take-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} as main {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, the following historical figures are additionally enshrined. * Minister Takeuchi ({{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) * Minister Toyotomi ({{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) * Lord Minamoto no Yoshiaki ({{ill|lt=Shiba Yoshimasa|Q11500863|qid=Q11500863|1=Shiba Yoshiyuki}}) * Great Arieke ({{ill|lt=Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817|qid=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}}) Also, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|qid=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} of nearby shrines in the Meiji period, the following deities are worshiped as well. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi deity|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okinaga-tarahime-no-Mikoto and Hondawake-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) * {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|qid=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) However, the 'Old shrine records and similar in Etchū Province' records the deity as Ōwakago-no-Mikoto. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto is another name of Ōhatahushi-no-Ōkami, main deity of {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|qid=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Chikuzen Province. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded by invitation from Takeuchi no Sukune in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. A local legend says a giant serpent lived in a large nearby pond and ate villagers. One time, when it ate a girl, the comb in her hair lodged in its throat and it died; worship of the girl and the comb is said to have begun this shrine. It is listed as "Kushida Shrine, Imizu District, Etchū Province" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Festivals === At the autumn annual festival, the {{ill|lt=fire-walking|Q929202|qid=Q929202|1=Firewalking}} rite is famous. === Other === Because stained-glass artist {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomo Fumiya|1=UNKNOWN}} from Imizu had deep attachment to this shrine in life, amulets embroidered with stained-glass motifs are produced. In the precincts, the "Ōtomo Fumiya Stained Glass Memorial Hall" (single-story reinforced concrete, about 90 m2, free admission) opened on {{ill|lt=December 2|Q2296|qid=Q2296|1=December 2}} {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|qid=Q2024|1=2007}}From Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 3, 2007, p.26: "Pioneer stained-glass works gathered at Daimon Kushida Shrine, Ōtomo Fumiya opens, 80 works stored and exhibited in sequence.". In summer {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}}, a lantern with stained glass was completed in front of the memorial hall[https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000009297 Stained-glass patterned amulets for first shrine visit: Imizu Kushida Shrine] (Hokuriku-Shinetsu Tourism Navi, 2016-12-27). In December 2019, to commemorate the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|qid=Q217096|lt=the current emperor|1=Naruhito}}, a new shinmon gate (height 3.6 m, width 5.2 m, depth 3.2 m) was constructed. Stained-glass lanterns were also installed beside itFrom Kita Nippon Shimbun, morning edition December 22, 2019, p.20: "Kushida Shrine builds new shinmon to celebrate emperor's enthronement; stained-glass lanterns also installed.". === Transportation === * Parking: available * Take {{ill|lt=Imizu City Community Bus|Q11458552|qid=Q11458552|1=Imizu City Community Bus}}, get off at "Matsubara Kominkan-mae," immediately nearby. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://kushida.main.jp/index.html Kushida Shrine official homepage] * {{Instagram|kushidajinja}} {{Commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Imizu}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=富山県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine, Imizu]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]]
